prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "跳跃前弓步" (jumping forward lunge) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person mid-air during a jumping forward lunge: airborne, both legs extended forward and back like in mid-stride; the front (right) leg is preparing to land in a deep lunge with knee starting to bend; the rear (left) leg is extended back with knee slightly bent; arms in running-style opposition; torso upright; explosive forward jumping motion; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps and gluteus maximus and calf muscles for jump propulsion. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group and hip flexors and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌 / 臀大肌" pointing to the quadriceps and glute area; "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles; "腘绳肌 / 核心" pointing to the hamstring and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
